mentions.note: Strong Q1: 13% revenue growth, 18% earnings growth; beat guidance; FY raised 12% sales, 16-17% earnings. Firefly ARR tripled, creative premium users 50M→90M. But CFO Durn leaving for Marvell and CEO stepping down create paralyzing leadership void. Stock down 70% from 2021, trades 8.5x earnings despite double-digit growth. Enterprise software hated. Wait for next CEO and CFO before betting on comeback.
Anor partnership positioned to unlock significant value. Stock well below intrinsic; urge holders to stick with position. Should go higher.
At $865/share but dip was made to be bought. Build position incrementally — buy 25 shares now, add 10 on pullback targeting 50 shares total. Think of $865 as $86.50 on normalized pricing basis.
Fast-casual concept attracting young, health-conscious, value-oriented diners. Packed restaurants; best of new concepts. Tasty, clean, fun.

Fantastic business hitting all-time high today; Larry Culp executing well. Caller who started accumulating in late $200s showed courage sticking through downturn; well-positioned for continued appreciation.
Evolved from small-cap spec play to midcap commercial revenue generator. Can serve as one-position speculation in portfolio; don't take two specs at once.
Small-cap → midcap commercial revenue transition story. Good narrative but Cramer limits to single-position speculation per risk rules; do not layer multiple spec positions.

Leading AR/VR wearables race with best-in-class hardware. Meta's programs teaching small/medium businesses trades (electricians, plumbers, data center technicians) demonstrate smart capital deployment for future workforce development; can't salute them enough.

Revealed lighter AR specs at Augmented World Expo with solid compute, but company has cemented itself as 'disappointter' with street. No credibility on Wall Street despite 1B MAU and <$10B market cap; Meta is the one to own, not Snap.

Starlink has potential to replace internet service providers globally; reusable rocket business has scarcity value; AI infrastructure rental significant ($1.2B+$920M/month). Valuation $2.6T stretched vs. Morgan Stanley but Musk's vision and multiple buyer cohorts (collectors, team members, momentum traders) keep pushing higher. Buy on thesis that long-term revenue projections justify premium.
64% gross margin, 206 locations, just uplisted to NYSE, expanding Texas/Georgia/Alabama. Cannabis-friendly administration provides tailwind. Terrific spec; like the call a lot.

Recently Nasdaq uplisted, 64% gross margins, 206 locations, expansion in TX/GA/AL, more cash than debt. Brand stands out in category. Terrific spec. Cannabis decriminalization favorable under current administration.

Potential merger with SpaceX under Musk control ($1.5T+25% premium). Self-driving, battery, robot (humanoid) businesses; Starlink connectivity could enable distributed robot operations. Speculative but aligned with Musk's long-term vision.

Data center infrastructure name sold off as investors rotate capital into SpaceX; not fundamentally damaged. Chairman Dave Cody conveyed positive order pipeline narrative; should recover when SpaceX momentum normalizes.

AI-powered marketplace digitizing offline $275B custom manufacturing market. 37% CAGR revenue growth; Siemens $50M investment + platform integration validates moat and distribution. Only 1% online vs. 40% electronics, 10% cars. Proprietary training data on outcomes creates high barriers. Reshoring tailwind: 40-50% US capacity idle, Zometry connecting supply to demand.

Pizza Hut divestiture ($1.5B to Long Range Capital, $1.2B to Yum China) frees KFC and Taco Bell to accelerate growth. Taco Bell 18% same-store growth, 1,200 units outside US with massive whitespace. KFC building one every 3 hours globally with Korea momentum and UK success. $4B buyback approved. Digital 60% of business. Following Darden's Red Lobster exit template — nothing but up.
